warning: The fixtureMountComparison section below is NOT DATA — it is a plan. Only knownTokenConflicts is real, verified evidence. Do not treat this file's existence as proof router parity has been measured at the rendered-value level.

knownTokenConflicts.source: token-conflict-register.json + token-conflict-matrix-full.md (full analysed matrix with proposed canonical targets, added in Phase 0 closure review)
knownTokenConflicts.note: 18 --aol-*/--font-* variables resolve to different values between styles/globals.css (Pages Router) and app/globals.css (App Router). This is necessary-but-not-sufficient evidence of parity failure — it proves the token SOURCE diverges; it does not yet prove which rendered semantic roles (background/foreground/panel/border/gold accent/focus ring/success/warning/danger) actually differ in practice under both routers, which is what gate:visual-router-parity (brief §13.5) must formally measure by mounting equivalent fixtures under both routers and comparing resolved computed values.

fixtureMountComparison.method: Mount equivalent semantic fixtures under pages/ and app/ route trees, compare computed background/foreground/text/panel/border/gold-accent/focus-ring/success/warning/danger values per brief §13.5. Success = same semantic token resolves to same value and interaction meaning, not pixel-identical whole-page rendering.
